<!-- Generated By: Claude Code (Claude Opus 4.7) -->

# Consuming Prometheus Exposition Output from Shell

Python's `prometheus_client` library prints every metric value as a float,
including counters and timestamps:

```text
claude_quota_fetch_failure_total 2.0
claude_quota_last_fetch_timestamp_seconds 1762534321.0
```

The `.0` suffix is part of the OpenMetrics exposition format — counters
are required to be expressible as floats. The format is correct on the
wire; the trap is on the consumer side when a shell-based scraper or
sidecar reads `/metrics` and feeds those values into POSIX `test` / `[`
arithmetic.

## Rule: Strip the decimal before integer comparison

POSIX `[ X -gt Y ]` rejects any operand that is not a strict integer:

```sh
# Wrong — fails with "[: 2.0: bad number"
failures=$(awk '/^claude_quota_fetch_failure_total/ {print $2}' metrics.txt)
if [ "$failures" -gt "$baseline" ]; then ...

# Right — parameter expansion truncates before the comparison
failures=$(awk '/^claude_quota_fetch_failure_total/ {print $2}' metrics.txt)
failures_int=${failures%.*}
if [ "$failures_int" -gt "$baseline" ]; then ...
```

`${var%.*}` is a POSIX parameter expansion (longest match of `.*` from the
right, returning the part before). No external command, works in
busybox / ash / dash / bash. The same form handles timestamps and any
other numeric exposition value.

## When to apply

- Any shell script that scrapes `/metrics` output produced by
  `prometheus_client` (Python) and uses the values in arithmetic or
  numeric comparisons.
- Other exposition formats may also emit floats for counters
  (`go-prometheus-client`, OpenMetrics text format in general). Default
  to the parameter-expansion truncation rather than assuming integer
  output.

## When skipping is acceptable

- The script only reads metric *names* / labels and never compares values.
- The script uses `awk` for the comparison itself (`awk` handles floats
  natively), in which case the truncation step is unnecessary.

## Related

- [standards/observability/metric-naming.md](https://github.com/jewzaam/standards/blob/main/observability/metric-naming.md)
  — the naming pattern these values flow through. The `.0` suffix appears
  regardless of name; the consumer rule applies to every numeric metric a
  `prometheus_client` exporter emits.
